% "The big table of the things that matter": annual labeling budget vs. delivered
% accuracy for each maintenance strategy on CESNET-TLS-Year22 (Week-16 ref, forward
% weeks 17-52, normalised to a 52-week year).
% Generated by scripts/analysis/labeling_cost_per_year.py
% Mean macro-F1 is on the softmax isolation panel (frozen forward F1). Ours = same-panel
% substitution of the MEASURED post-repair F1 of the detected teleported classes
% (49 docker 0.86, 57 eset 0.88, 140 skype 0.43; results/repair/joint_repair_v01) at
% their degraded weeks -> +0.009 (lower bound: credits only the measured teleporters).
% Retrain rows are IDEALISED (recover to ~peak each retrain, week-16 decay shape).
% MFWDD retrain cadence is threshold-dependent (3/6/10/14 at n_sigma=0.5/1/1.5/2).
% Full retrain = 180 x ~46k = ~8.3M labeled flows (week-16 train.parquet = 8.26M rows).
\begin{table}[t]
\centering
\caption{One year of catalogue maintenance: \emph{labeled flows spent} vs.\
\emph{mean macro-F1 delivered} (CESNET-TLS-Year22, 180 classes, Week-16 reference,
normalised to 52 weeks). Blind adaptation is free but moves accuracy $\le 0$; global
retraining recovers accuracy but at millions of labels and cannot say which class
broke; our monitor spends a few dozen labels per flagged class, lifting global
macro-F1 (and restoring the broken classes' per-class recall from ${\sim}0$ to
$0.86$--$0.88$) at $0.06$--$0.30\%$ of a single retrain.}
\label{tab:labelcost}
\setlength{\tabcolsep}{4pt}
\begin{tabular}{@{}lrr@{}}
\hline
\textbf{Strategy} & \textbf{Labeled flows/yr} & \textbf{Mean macro-F1} \\
\hline
Do nothing (frozen source)            & $0$      & $0.686$ \\
UDA/TTA (DANN, TENT, CoTTA)            & $0$      & $\le 0.686^{\,a}$ \\
\textbf{Ours} (monitor\,+\,few-shot, $K{=}10$) & $\mathbf{4.9}$\textbf{k} & $\mathbf{0.694}^{\,b}$ \\
\textbf{Ours} (monitor\,+\,few-shot, $K{=}50$) & $\mathbf{25}$\textbf{k}  & $\mathbf{0.694}^{\,b}$ \\
MFWDD $\rightarrow$ retrain (${\sim}6\times$/yr)$^{\,c}$ & $50$M & $0.785^{\,d}$ \\
Periodic full retrain (monthly)       & $99$M    & $0.800^{\,d}$ \\
Full-recovery ceiling (retrain weekly)$^{\,d}$ & ---     & $0.829$ \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\\[2pt]
\footnotesize{Ground truth: $1847$ degraded class-weeks / $266$ degradation episodes
($136/180$ classes). Our $25$k/yr ($K{=}50$) already \emph{includes} acting on the
monitor's $4.9\%$ false alarms (${\sim}191$/yr); the budget is robust to modelling
choices (label-shift false alarms $\to0.82\%$ of a retrain; worst case, $K$ per
degraded class-week, $\to1.73\%$). The accuracy column is conservative for us in
\emph{both} directions: the $+0.009$ global lift credits only the \emph{measured}
repair of the detected teleporters (their own per-class recall jumps ${\sim}0\to0.86$),
while the label budget counts the \emph{full} monitor cost.\\
$^{a}$DANN forward $\Delta\text{acc}{=}{+}0.006$; TENT/CoTTA negative (Table~\ref{tab:nt_merged}).
$^{b}$$K{=}10$ shares the $K{=}50$ lift (measured at $K{=}50$); the label cost differs, the F1 estimate does not.
$^{c}$MFWDD cannot isolate \emph{which} class broke (per-class AUROC $0.72$ vs ours $0.88$;
recall caps at $0.62$), so each alarm forces a full retrain; as a global weekly drift
detector its cadence is threshold-dependent (order $5$--$10\times$/yr).
$^{d}$Retrain accuracies are \emph{idealised} (each retrain recovers to ${\sim}$peak and
re-decays along the Week-16 drift shape) -- an optimistic upper bound for retraining.}
\end{table}
